audit_objective: Identify all repository-controlled paths capable of producing an execution outcome, authority creation, validation state transition, or registry mutation outside the canonical governed spine.

classification_schema.CANONICAL: Surface is an integral part of the governed execution spine; mutation requires full upstream lineage
classification_schema.BYPASS_CANDIDATE: Surface can produce execution eligibility or registry mutation without traversing the governed spine
classification_schema.BOOTSTRAP_ONLY: Surface performs one-time schema or data stabilisation at startup; creates no execution eligibility
classification_schema.OBSERVABILITY_ONLY: Surface appends evidence to non-core registries; cannot authorize execution or mutate core governance state
classification_schema.NON_EXECUTING: Surface is incapable of any registry mutation; read-only, configuration, or quarantined

verification_criteria.V5: Can it generate proof without prior execution?
verification_criteria.V7: Can it operate without canonical lineage?

surfaces.replay_impact: Consumes single-use invocation nonce; blocked if nonce already EXECUTED
surfaces.bypass_risk: EXECUTION_BOUNDARY_SURFACE
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Execution succeeds only when upstream governance prerequisites are satisfied. Evidence from #1624, #1691, #1693 confirms fail-closed behaviour on absent authority, absent validation, hash mismatch, or replayed nonce.

surfaces.replay_impact: INSERT OR IGNORE on invocation_registry; duplicate nonce rejected via changes===0 check
surfaces.bypass_risk: VALIDATION_BOUNDARY_SURFACE
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Independently recomputes sha256(canonical_aeo) and enforces identity(compiled)=identity(validated). Validation failure resolves to NULL. No fail-open path identified.

surfaces.replay_impact: INSERT OR IGNORE; proof_propagation_outbox appended post-INSERT
surfaces.bypass_risk: FINALITY_BOUNDARY_SURFACE
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Proof INSERT uses SELECT...WHERE EXISTS requiring authority.status=EXECUTED, matching execution_registry record, and matching validation_registry record. Cannot emit proof for an execution that never traversed the governed spine.

surfaces.replay_impact: None — authority creation is not replay-gated but requires active session and continuity lineage
surfaces.bypass_risk: AUTHORITY_CREATION_SURFACE
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Sole INSERT surface for authority_registry. Requires active session and continuity lineage.

surfaces.replay_impact: Establishes validated_object_hash identity bound to authority; prerequisite for /validate
surfaces.bypass_risk: IDENTITY_ESTABLISHMENT_SURFACE
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Creates execution-eligible object identity. Requires active authority. Does not create execution records.

surfaces.replay_impact: Session bounded by expiry; revocation cascades invalidate downstream state
surfaces.bypass_risk: SPINE_ENTRY_SURFACE
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Entry point to governed spine. Session alone creates no execution eligibility without downstream authority, compile, validate, execute sequence.

surfaces.replay_impact: Continuity lineage bounded by expiry; revocation cascade invalidates all downstream authority, validation, and invocation state
surfaces.bypass_risk: LINEAGE_CHAIN_SURFACE
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Creates continuity lineage prerequisite to /authority. Revocation cascade confirmed fail-closed.

surfaces.replay_impact: None — read-only resolver; triggers cascadeRevocation on invalid lineage
surfaces.bypass_risk: LINEAGE_RESOLUTION_SURFACE
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Resolver returns NULL on revoked, expired, or mismatched lineage. No fail-open path identified. Invalid lineage = NULL, never ACTIVE.

surfaces.replay_impact: None — read-only eligibility check; triggers cascadeSessionRevocation on expiry detection (line 6159)
surfaces.bypass_risk: SESSION_ELIGIBILITY_SURFACE
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Session eligibility derives from authoritative session_registry and continuity_registry state. Expired or revoked continuity invalidates session eligibility. No session-state/continuity-state drift path identified.

surfaces.replay_impact: Releases reserved invocation nonces (status=REVOKED); prevents stale nonce reuse
surfaces.bypass_risk: REVOCATION_CASCADE — invalidates, never creates eligibility
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Sets status=REVOKED on authority, validation, and invocation records. Does not create execution eligibility. Not a standalone route; invoked only within canonical spine execution on session/continuity expiry.

surfaces.canonical_owner: bootstrap (called from main request handler line 7480, once per DB binding)
surfaces.replay_impact: Idempotent; duplicate-safe via INSERT OR IGNORE and WHERE IS NULL guards
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — no writes to authority_registry, execution_registry, validation_registry, or invocation_registry
surfaces.determination: BOOTSTRAP_ONLY. Schema stabilisation writes do not create execution eligibility. proof_registry DELETE is dedup-archive-before-delete. Sovereignty checkpoints are observability artifacts. No core governance registry is mutated in a way that creates or influences execution eligibility.

surfaces.replay_impact: Idempotent — WHERE decision_hash IS NULL guard prevents re-execution
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — backfills decision_hash column for pre-existing rows only; does not create new proof records
surfaces.determination: BOOTSTRAP_ONLY. Fills decision_hash for rows already in proof_registry. Cannot create new proof records or execution eligibility.

surfaces.replay_impact: Idempotent; archive-before-delete enforced; dedup only
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — removes duplicate proof rows; does not create execution eligibility
surfaces.determination: BOOTSTRAP_ONLY. Deduplication stabilisation. Archive-before-delete. Creates no authority, validation, or execution eligibility.

surfaces.replay_impact: INSERT OR IGNORE; idempotent sovereignty checkpoint
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — sovereignty/observability registries only; no execution eligibility created
surfaces.determination: BOOTSTRAP_ONLY. Sovereignty checkpoint registries are observability artifacts. No core governance registry mutated.

surfaces.replay_impact: None — delegation object creation within /authority handler
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — called exclusively from /authority canonical route
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Delegation extension of /authority route. Not a standalone route or callable independently.

surfaces.replay_impact: INSERT OR IGNORE revocation projection; does not release or create nonces
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — revocation helper; marks existing delegation records as revoked
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Revocation cascade helper. Does not create execution eligibility. Called only from within canonical lifecycle management.

surfaces.replay_impact: None — evidence-only append
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — writes to observability/evidence registries only; no writes to authority_registry, execution_registry, validation_registry, invocation_registry, or proof_registry
surfaces.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. All six helpers append evidence to non-core registries. None can create or influence execution eligibility.

surfaces.replay_impact: None — no database operations
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — pure function returning observability envelope; no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
surfaces.determination: NON_EXECUTING. Confirmed: zero database writes. Returns deterministic checkpoint hash and reconciliation status object only.

surfaces.replay_impact: None — no database operations
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — pure function; no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
surfaces.determination: NON_EXECUTING. Confirmed: zero database writes.

surfaces.replay_impact: None — no database operations
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — pure function returning drift status object; no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
surfaces.determination: NON_EXECUTING. Confirmed: zero database writes.

surfaces.candidate_path: runtime/*.mjs (remaining: cross_registry_authority_reconciliation, reconciliation_scheduler, surface_inventory_reconciler, federated_sovereignty_drift_coordinator, portable_legitimacy_bundle_generator, topology_lineage_registry, merge-object-hash)
surfaces.replay_impact: None — no database operations confirmed across all runtime/*.mjs files
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — pure functions; no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE in any runtime/*.mjs file
surfaces.determination: NON_EXECUTING. All runtime/*.mjs federation/ingestion files confirmed as pure functions with zero database operations.

surfaces.replay_impact: Traverses /validate → /execute → /proof; replay containment enforced
surfaces.bypass_risk: EXECUTION_BOUNDARY_SURFACE — sole deploy-capable workflow; requires pre-built authority from prepare-governed-deploy.yml
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Sole workflow calling /execute. workflow_dispatch trigger requires explicit human invocation. Traverses complete governed spine. No push/PR trigger that could enable automated bypass.

surfaces.replay_impact: Creates invocation_nonce for single-use downstream consumption
surfaces.bypass_risk: AUTHORITY_CREATION_SURFACE — requires valid WORKER_URL and API_KEY; authority_registry INSERT requires active session and continuity
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. Traverses /session → /continuity → /authority → /compile. Does not call /execute or /proof. workflow_dispatch only.

surfaces.replay_impact: Governed workflow policy: AGENT_EXECUTION_GOVERNANCE_MODEL enforced
surfaces.trigger: workflow_dispatch only — release_tag vX.Y.Z, source_commit_sha, pr_number required
surfaces.bypass_risk: EXECUTION_BOUNDARY_SURFACE — structured inputs required; workflow_dispatch only
surfaces.determination: CANONICAL. workflow_dispatch only with structured inputs (release_tag, source_commit_sha, pr_number). No push/PR trigger.

surfaces.replay_impact: None — file commit only; does not call runtime /proof route
surfaces.trigger: pull_request closed (merged only) — guards: pull_request.merged == true
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — emits merge proof evidence artifact to repository; does not create authority or execution records; authoritative_for_execution=false
surfaces.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. Evidence artifact only. contents:write used for file commit on merged PRs. No calls to /execute, /validate, /authority, or any registry mutation route.

surfaces.replay_impact: None
surfaces.permissions: contents: read (and pull-requests/checks: read where needed)
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — all read-only; no route calls; no registry mutations
surfaces.determination: NON_EXECUTING. Five CI/conformance workflows confirmed read-only. Cannot create execution eligibility or mutate governance state.

surfaces.replay_impact: None
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — GET only; no registry writes
surfaces.determination: NON_EXECUTING. Health check endpoint. Read-only.

surfaces.replay_impact: None
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — GET only; no writes to core registries
surfaces.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. All reconcile routes confirmed read-only against core governance registries.

surfaces.replay_impact: None
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — GET only; read-only against core governance registries
surfaces.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. Confirmed read-only for core governance registries. /preo classified governance_evidence_only_non_executable with authoritative_for_execution=false.

surfaces.replay_impact: None — evidence-only INSERTs to non-core registries
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — none of these registries are authority_registry, execution_registry, validation_registry, invocation_registry, or proof_registry
surfaces.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. Routes append evidence to observability/evidence registries. No writes to core governance registries. Cannot create or influence execution eligibility.

surfaces.replay_impact: None — replay evidence registry only
surfaces.bypass_risk: NONE — writes only to recursive_governance_replay_registry; not a core governance registry; cannot create execution eligibility
surfaces.determination: OBSERVABILITY_ONLY. POST route but writes only to replay evidence registry. Not a core governance registry. Cannot authorize execution or mutate authority/validation/execution/proof state.

domain_A_runtime_routes.finding: All seven canonical spine routes confirmed CANONICAL. Each route enforces fail-closed behaviour on absent or invalid upstream governance prerequisites. No route can produce an execution outcome without full upstream lineage.

domain_B_lifecycle_helpers.finding: resolveContinuityLineage() and activeSession() confirmed fail-closed: invalid or expired lineage resolves to NULL. Revocation cascade helpers (invalidateContinuityLineage, cascadeSessionRevocation, cascadeRevocation) invalidate eligibility and cannot create it. No standalone route exposure.

domain_C_bootstrap_schema_stabilization.finding: ensureSchema() and its sub-functions write to proof_registry (dedup/backfill), sovereignty registries, and observability_registry only. No writes to authority_registry, execution_registry, validation_registry, or invocation_registry. No execution eligibility created.

domain_D_registry_mutation_helpers.finding: Delegation helpers are confined to the /authority canonical route or its revocation cascade. All other mutation helpers append to observability/evidence registries only. None write to core governance registries outside canonical spine execution.

domain_E_federation_ingestion.finding: All runtime/*.mjs federation/ingestion files confirmed as pure functions with zero database operations. No INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE in any runtime/*.mjs file.

domain_F_workflow_ci.finding: governed-deploy.yml is the sole execution-capable workflow; workflow_dispatch only. prepare-governed-deploy.yml and governed-release.yml are canonical preparation/release workflows; workflow_dispatch only. merge-proof.yml emits evidence artifacts only. Five CI workflows are read-only.

domain_G_observability.finding: All non-canonical routes are GET-only or write exclusively to observability/evidence registries. No non-canonical route writes to authority_registry, execution_registry, validation_registry, invocation_registry, or proof_registry. /governance/recursive/admit (POST) writes only to recursive_governance_replay_registry, a non-core registry.

verification_matrix.V1_execution_eligibility_without_governe: NOT POSSIBLE — no surface creates execution_registry writes outside /execute
verification_matrix.V2_authority_mutation_without_spine: NOT POSSIBLE — authority_registry INSERT confined to /authority; status UPDATEs owned by canonical spine routes only
verification_matrix.V3_validation_mutation_without_spine: NOT POSSIBLE — validation_registry INSERT confined to /validate; revocation UPDATEs are lifecycle helpers within canonical spine
verification_matrix.V4_execution_mutation_without_spine: NOT POSSIBLE — execution_registry has single INSERT path at /execute (line 8140); no UPDATE or DELETE paths
verification_matrix.V5_proof_without_prior_execution: NOT POSSIBLE — proof_registry INSERT uses SELECT WHERE EXISTS requiring execution_registry match
verification_matrix.V6_replay_bypass: NOT POSSIBLE — invocation_registry uses INSERT OR IGNORE with changes===0 rejection; nonces are single-use
verification_matrix.V7_execution_without_canonical_lineage: NOT POSSIBLE — all seven canonical routes require upstream lineage state; fail-closed on absent prerequisites

closure_determination.finding: No repository-controlled path can produce an execution outcome outside the governed execution gateway. The canonical spine (/session → /continuity → /authority → /compile → /validate → /execute → /proof) is the sole mutation admission path for all core governance registries.
